companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: VivaTech 2026 / GTC Paris continues through June 20 at Paris Expo Porte de Versailles. Jensen Huang spotlighted European sovereign AI factories — including partnerships with Telenor and Telefónica — and cited Mistral AI as the anchor of France's sovereign compute strategy. NVIDIA and CMU/UC Berkeley also published ENPIRE, a closed-loop framework that hands the full robotics research cycle to AI coding agents operating on real hardware.

items.headline: Jensen Huang highlighted European sovereign AI progress at VivaTech GTC Paris
items.detail: At the GTC Paris keynote (June 17, VivaTech 2026), Huang reported on the 20+ AI factories promised for Europe at VivaTech 2025, citing new sovereign AI partnerships with Telenor in Norway and Telefónica in Spain. Mistral AI was named the flagship French sovereign compute partner, with NVIDIA's DSX platform providing the reference design for each national factory.

items.headline: NVIDIA, CMU, and UC Berkeley released ENPIRE — a closed-loop AI agent framework for robotics research
items.detail: NVIDIA published ENPIRE on June 17, a framework that delegates the full robotics research cycle — from experiment design to physical hardware execution — to AI coding agents. Jensen Huang highlighted Isaac GR00T N1.5, a foundational model for humanoid robot reasoning, as now available for download alongside ENPIRE.

items.headline: NVIDIA's Vera CPUs are in production at Anthropic, OpenAI, SpaceXAI, and Oracle Cloud
items.detail: NVIDIA confirmed Vera CPUs entered production at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI, with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure committing to hundreds of thousands of units starting in 2026. Broader availability across AWS, Google Cloud, Azure, Lambda, and Nebius is expected in H2 2026.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash reached general availability as Google's most capable agentic model, outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%) and GDPval-AA (1656 Elo). Google simultaneously launched Managed Agents in the Gemini API in public preview — stateful, autonomous agents running in Google-hosted Linux sandboxes — and announced deprecation of Gemini 3.1 Flash Image and Gemini 3 Pro Image preview models, with shutdown on June 25. G7 outcomes at Évian produced no binding AI regulatory framework; the nine declarations adopted were described as 'a conversation rather than a negotiation.'

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash went generally available with Managed Agents and the Antigravity agent in public preview
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Flash reached GA as the frontier model for agentic and coding tasks. Google simultaneously launched Managed Agents in the Gemini API — stateful agents running in Google-hosted Linux sandboxes — and the 'Antigravity' general-purpose agent capable of multi-step planning, coding, file management, and web browsing.

items.headline: Google deprecated Gemini 3.1 Flash Image and Gemini 3 Pro Image preview models — shutdown June 25
items.detail: Google announced that gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and gemini-3-pro-image-preview are deprecated and will be shut down on June 25, 2026. Developers are directed to migrate to Gemini 3.5 Flash.

items.headline: G7 Évian summit adopted nine AI declarations but reached no binding regulatory framework
items.detail: The G7 summit concluded June 15–17 in Évian-les-Bains with nine AI declarations covering child safety standards and beneficial AI deployment. AI lab CEOs Hassabis, Altman, and Amodei attended but the summit produced no binding framework — characterized by observers as 'a conversation rather than a negotiation.' A 'trusted partners' platform for vetted allied-nation AI access is to be established within one month, with a September follow-on meeting.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Work IQ APIs went generally available on June 16, completing the initial Microsoft IQ rollout announced at Build 2026 and giving third-party agents programmatic access to organizational intelligence inside the M365 trust boundary. Claude Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5, and Opus 4.1 entered public preview in Microsoft Foundry, joining GPT-5.5 (GA on June 3). Microsoft Copilot has surpassed 5 million paid seats, with Copilot Business Standard and Copilot Business Premium becoming permanent SKUs on July 1.

items.headline: Work IQ APIs and Web IQ went generally available, completing the Microsoft IQ intelligence layer rollout
items.detail: Microsoft's Work IQ APIs — providing agents programmatic access to organizational context inside the M365 trust boundary — went GA on June 16 alongside Web IQ, described as the fastest real-world grounding available for agents. Together they complete the initial Microsoft IQ intelligence layer rollout from Build 2026.

items.headline: Claude Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5, and Opus 4.1 entered public preview in Microsoft Foundry
items.detail: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5, and Opus 4.1 are now in public preview in Microsoft Foundry, expanding the model choices available to enterprise developers building agents on Azure. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 had reached general availability in Foundry on June 3.

items.headline: Microsoft Copilot surpassed 5 million paid seats; Business Standard and Premium become permanent SKUs July 1
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot crossed 5 million paid seats driven by a landmark NHS contract and declining Azure AI infrastructure costs. Copilot Business Standard and Copilot Business Premium will become permanent commercial SKUs effective July 1, 2026, locking in the two-tier enterprise structure.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, with timing described as 'not yet determined.' GPT-5.6 is generating significant pre-announcement attention — OpenAI chief scientist Jakub Pachocki described it internally as a 'meaningful improvement' over GPT-5.5, with prediction markets placing ~83% probability on a June 22–28 release window at over $960,000 in volume. The $150 million Partner Network (launched June 14–16) and the Ona acquisition continue as pre-IPO enterprise distribution buildout.

items.headline: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 SEC filing on June 8; timing 'not yet determined'
items.detail: OpenAI publicly confirmed filing a confidential S-1 with the SEC on June 8, following Anthropic's June 1 filing. Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan are leading the offering. The company said IPO timing has 'not yet been determined' — a more cautious posture than earlier September–Q4 2026 window reporting.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 described internally as a 'meaningful improvement'; prediction markets at ~83% for a late-June release
items.detail: OpenAI chief scientist Jakub Pachocki described GPT-5.6 as a 'meaningful improvement' over GPT-5.5 in internal communications. Prediction market traders have placed over $960,000 in volume assigning approximately 83% implied probability to a June 22–28 release window. No official model card or benchmarks have been published.

items.headline: OpenAI launched a $150 million Partner Network to certify 300,000 AI specialists
items.detail: OpenAI introduced the Partner Network on June 14–16, committing $150 million and engaging firms including Accenture and Bain to certify 300,000 AI specialists across enterprise workflows — a structured go-to-market expansion ahead of the planned IPO.

items.headline: OpenAI confirmed the acquisition of Ona (formerly Gitpod) to give Codex persistent long-running cloud sessions
items.detail: OpenAI announced the acquisition of Ona on June 11–12, whose technology enables agents to access tools, systems, and context over extended periods without remaining tied to a single active session. The addition lets Codex users delegate work that may take hours or days.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain offline globally as of June 18 — but co-founder Jack Clark told Bloomberg the company is now in daily discussions with the Trump administration to resolve the dispute. Clark outlined Anthropic's vision for AI safety in a June 18 Bloomberg interview, signaling active diplomatic progress. The Pentagon has reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic's models. Fortune identified the government's ability to suspend model access as Anthropic's primary IPO risk. On the product side, Anthropic shipped Claude Workload Identity Federation (keyless authentication across API/SDK/Claude Code) and Claude Design updates; a service disruption affecting thousands of users was reported around 3 AM ET on June 18.

items.headline: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain globally offline as of June 18; no restoration date announced
items.detail: The US Commerce Department's export control directive ordering suspension of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals — issued June 12 — remains in effect. Because Anthropic cannot segregate foreign nationals in real time, both models are offline globally for all users. Anthropic dispatched senior technical staff to Washington; as of June 17–18 no official reinstatement has been announced.

items.headline: G7 Évian agreed to establish a 'trusted partners' AI platform within one month, September follow-on planned
items.detail: G7 members agreed at Évian to establish a 'trusted partners' exemption platform within one month — aimed at allowing vetted allied-nation entities to regain access to Fable 5 and similar frontier models — with leaders planning a September follow-on meeting to finalize terms. US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick led the pitch; French President Macron cited the Anthropic suspension as evidence of US AI 'kill switch' fears.

items.headline: Fortune identified the government's demonstrated power to suspend model access as Anthropic's primary IPO risk
items.detail: Fortune's June 16 analysis of Anthropic's confidential S-1 concluded that the government's demonstrated ability to suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access represents a novel material IPO risk — one Anthropic must disclose in its public S-1 and that investors must price into a government-regulated AI company.

items.headline: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark said the company is in daily discussions with the Trump administration to resolve Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls
items.detail: In a June 18 Bloomberg interview, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark confirmed active daily dialogue with the Trump administration. Clark declined to share specifics but said Anthropic and the government share a goal of addressing national security challenges — signaling the most concrete diplomatic progress since the June 12 suspension. Clark also outlined Anthropic's broader vision for bolstering AI model safety.

items.headline: Anthropic shipped Claude Workload Identity Federation and Claude Design updates
items.detail: Anthropic released Workload Identity Federation for the Claude Platform — generally available keyless authentication across API endpoints, SDKs, and Claude Code. Claude Design also shipped updates: tighter Claude Code sync, direct canvas editing, stronger layout controls, and more tool connections for smoother design-to-code handoff. A separate service disruption was reported by thousands of users around 3 AM ET on June 18.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta launched 'AI Mode' on Facebook on June 15 — a new search experience that synthesizes answers from public posts across Groups and Reels. Separately, Meta's internal AI token-cost crisis became public: employees consumed 73.7 trillion tokens in ~30 days on a leaderboard called 'Claudeonomics,' prompting Meta to announce 'AI Gateway,' a centralized spending cap and monitoring platform. The Jamnagar, India data center (168 MW, Reliance Industries) remains under construction. 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion is unchanged.

items.headline: Meta leased a 168 MW AI data center from Reliance Industries in Jamnagar, India
items.detail: Meta announced it will lease an AI-enabled data center in Jamnagar, India from Reliance Industries (168 MW capacity), with Reliance building and delivering the facility within two years and an option to scale. Meta is also contracting nearly 1 GW of renewable energy from Indian providers CleanMax and Fourth Partner Energy for the facility.

items.headline: Meta launched 'AI Mode' on Facebook — a new search experience synthesizing answers from public posts
items.detail: Meta introduced AI Mode on Facebook on June 15, letting users ask questions in plain language and receive synthesized answers drawn from public posts, Groups, and Reels across the platform. The feature uses Meta AI to surface community-sourced knowledge rather than static web results — positioning Facebook as an AI-powered information layer for Meta's 3.2 billion daily active users.

items.headline: Meta capped internal AI token spending after employees consumed 73.7 trillion tokens in a month
items.detail: Meta's internal AI-usage leaderboard 'Claudeonomics' revealed employees consumed 73.7 trillion tokens in roughly 30 days, driving costs toward billions annually. Meta announced it will dismantle the leaderboard and replace it with a centralized 'AI Gateway' platform enforcing per-team budgets and real-time spend monitoring. The episode highlighted a broader tech-industry tension between AI productivity mandates and ballooning token costs as agentic AI consumes up to 1,000× more tokens than standard AI.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit New York wrapped its two-day run (June 17–18) at the Javits Center. Day 2 featured deeper AgentCore capability announcements: a Managed Knowledge Base with Smart Parsing and Agentic Retriever, a fully managed Web Search tool for agent grounding, Agent Optimization with A/B testing and intent/trajectory analytics, and Bedrock Guardrails integration for prompt injection detection. Security integrations with Check Point, Zscaler, Rubrik, Netskope, and SentinelOne are coming soon. Southwest Airlines announced AWS as its preferred cloud partner, committing to a cloud- and agent-native architecture by 2028.

items.headline: AgentCore at AWS Summit gained Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, Agent Optimization, and Guardrails
items.detail: Day 2 of AWS Summit New York detailed new Amazon Bedrock AgentCore capabilities: a Managed Knowledge Base with Smart Parsing and Agentic Retriever for enterprise RAG; a fully managed Web Search tool grounding agents in current web knowledge with zero data egress; Agent Optimization with A/B testing and failure/intent/trajectory insights; and Bedrock Guardrails integration for prompt injection detection.

items.headline: Kiro IDE highlighted at Summit as applying aerospace-grade specification standards to AI-assisted coding
items.detail: AWS showcased Kiro at the NYC Summit — the new agentic IDE that generates requirements.md, design.md, and tasks.md specs using aerospace-grade EARS notation before writing code, with each session running inside a Firecracker microVM. Kiro Pro Max tier with higher usage limits and frontier-model access is now available for professional development teams.

items.headline: Southwest Airlines named AWS preferred cloud partner, committing to cloud- and agent-native architecture by 2028
items.detail: Southwest Airlines announced AWS as its preferred cloud partner at the NYC Summit, committing to transition from a largely on-premises environment to a cloud- and agent-enabled architecture by 2028 — the latest major enterprise named alongside Amazon's growing AI infrastructure footprint.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) is trading around $191.82 on June 18 — down from its June 16 all-time high of $225.64 — after announcing the $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI). On the model distribution side, Grok 4.3 went generally available on Amazon Bedrock on June 15 with a 1-million-token context window and configurable reasoning effort; and on June 18 at the Databricks Data + AI Summit, xAI announced native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks. The Devin Kim whistleblower lawsuit over Grok safety concerns continues in Santa Clara County Superior Court.

items.headline: SpaceX confirmed a $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI) on June 16
items.detail: SpaceX confirmed it will acquire Anysphere, the parent company of AI coding tool Cursor, for $60 billion in an all-stock deal — just four days after completing the $75 billion SpaceX IPO. The deal expands SpaceXAI's developer tools alongside the Grok models and xAI data centers already absorbed in the February 2026 merger.

items.headline: SPCX trades around $191.82 on June 18, down from $225.64 all-time high after a hawkish Fed signal
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) debuted at $135 on June 12, surged to an all-time high of $225.64 on June 16, then pulled back to approximately $191.82 on June 18 (day range $187.01–$213.80) following a hawkish Federal Reserve signal. The stock is still up approximately 42% from its IPO price in six trading days.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 went generally available on Amazon Bedrock with a 1-million-token context window
items.detail: xAI's Grok 4.3 became generally available on Amazon Bedrock on June 15, 2026. The model runs on Mantle — Amazon's new inference engine optimized for price-performance — and features a 1-million-token context window, configurable reasoning effort (none/low/medium/high), the lowest hallucination rate among frontier models in Amazon's benchmarks, and full support for tool calling, structured output, and response streaming.

items.headline: Grok became natively available on Databricks Agent Bricks at the 2026 Data + AI Summit
items.detail: At the Databricks 2026 Data + AI Summit on June 18, SpaceXAI and Databricks announced native Grok availability in Databricks Agent Bricks. Grok models connect directly to context stored in the Databricks Lakehouse, eliminating a separate API integration and making Grok available alongside other frontier and open-source models in a single governed platform.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: AVGO is trading around $392.90 on June 18 — rebounding after falling ~12% following Q2 FY2026 earnings when Q3 AI revenue guidance of $16 billion came in below analyst consensus of $17.2 billion. JPMorgan rates the stock an 'aggressive buy' and 48 analysts have a 'Strong Buy' consensus with a $522 target. An ex-dividend date of $0.65 per share is set for June 22. Full-year FY2026 AI chip revenue target remains $56 billion (~180% growth), with a $100 billion+ FY2027 target across six core custom-silicon customers.

items.headline: Broadcom Q2 FY2026 AI chip revenue hit $10.8B (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance of $16B came in below estimates
items.detail: Broadcom reported Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion, up 143% year over year, on total consolidated revenue of $22.2 billion (up 48% YoY). The Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance of $16 billion came in below analyst consensus of $17.2 billion, sending shares down approximately 12% in after-hours trading on June 3.

items.headline: AVGO rebounding to ~$392.90 on June 18; ex-dividend date June 22; JPMorgan rates 'aggressive buy'
items.detail: Broadcom (AVGO) is trading around $392.90 (+4.30% recently), recovering after the post-earnings drop. JPMorgan rates AVGO an 'aggressive buy' and 48 analysts hold a 'Strong Buy' consensus with a 12-month price target of $522. A $0.65 cash dividend has an ex-date of June 22, 2026.

items.headline: Broadcom unveiled Tomahawk 6 (Davisson), the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics
items.detail: Broadcom announced the Tomahawk 6 (Davisson), the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics, designed to meet the bandwidth demands of AI data centers where conventional pluggable optics are insufficient at scale.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Maven Smart System was designated an official DoD program of record in 2026, locking it into the Future Years Defense Program with protected multi-year funding across the Army, Air Force, Space Force, Navy, and Marine Corps. Palantir reported record Q1 FY2026 results — adjusted EPS of $0.33 (vs. $0.28 consensus), revenue $1.632 billion up 84.7% YoY — and raised full-year guidance to ~71% growth. The stock fell 3.08% on June 12 after a £50 million UK Metropolitan Police contract was blocked, then rebounded 5.25% on June 15.

items.headline: Maven Smart System was designated an official DoD program of record, securing multi-year protected funding
items.detail: Palantir's Maven Smart System — an AI-powered targeting platform integrating computer vision across satellite and drone feeds — was formally designated an official DoD program of record in 2026, locking it into the Future Years Defense Program with protected multi-year funding across five military branches.

items.headline: Palantir reported Q1 FY2026 EPS of $0.33 and revenue up 84.7% YoY — the highest growth rate in company history
items.detail: Palantir's Q1 FY2026 results showed adjusted EPS of $0.33 vs. $0.28 consensus on revenue of $1.632 billion, up 84.7% YoY — the highest growth rate in company history — with US commercial revenue surging 133% to $595 million. Management raised full-year guidance to roughly 71% growth.

items.headline: PLTR fell on a blocked UK Metropolitan Police contract, then rebounded 5.25% on June 15
items.detail: Palantir fell 3.08% on June 12 after a £50 million UK Metropolitan Police AI contract was blocked by regulators and NHS deal scrutiny intensified, but shares recovered 5.25% on June 15 as investors weighed record Q1 results against near-term regulatory headwinds.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 developer beta is live with a rebuilt Siri AI (persistent memory, on-screen awareness, Visual Intelligence). The iOS 27 Extensions framework letting users choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri is built but disabled pending DMA resolution and legal clarity with OpenAI. A new development: President Trump stated publicly that Intel will manufacture chips for Apple — a potential shift from Apple's primary reliance on TSMC that sent Intel stock sharply higher on June 18. Siri AI will not launch in the EU or China at iOS 27's fall release.

items.headline: Apple introduced Siri AI at WWDC 2026 with persistent memory, on-screen awareness, and Visual Intelligence
items.detail: Apple unveiled Siri AI at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — a fully rebuilt Siri with personal context memory across conversations and sessions, on-screen awareness of what is on screen, expanded Visual Intelligence, and integrated writing tools. Siri AI will not launch in the EU at iOS 27's fall release due to unresolved European Commission DMA negotiations, and will not launch simultaneously in China.

items.headline: The iOS 27 Extensions framework supporting ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini inside Siri is built but disabled
items.detail: Apple's iOS 27 developer beta contains a working Extensions framework that would let users choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri with a dedicated settings panel and App Store section. Apple has disabled the feature on the backend pending DMA regulatory clarity in the EU and legal resolution with OpenAI, which is exploring a breach-of-contract action over perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement.
items.sourceTitle: The Next Web / Let's Data Science

items.headline: President Trump stated Intel will manufacture chips for Apple, sending Intel stock sharply higher
items.detail: President Trump said publicly on June 18 that Intel will produce chips for Apple — a potential shift from Apple's near-exclusive reliance on TSMC for its most advanced silicon. Intel stock surged on the report as investors weighed whether the deal would apply to future iPhone or Mac chips. Apple and Intel have not officially confirmed specifics; Trump's statement was made in the context of the administration's domestic manufacturing push.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD has begun sampling MI450 GPUs to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta, and is already in early engagement with customers on MI500 planning. The company confirmed its Advancing AI 2026 event for July 23, expected to provide deeper MI400 series technical disclosures. AMD recently completed the MEXT acquisition (June 15) for predictive memory technology and signed a 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment agreement with Rackspace (June 16). Citi upgraded AMD to Buy at $575 on June 12 citing confidence in the 12 GW combined AI GPU roadmap.

items.headline: AMD confirmed MI450 GPU sampling is underway and early customer engagement on MI500 has begun
items.detail: AMD CEO Dr. Lisa Su confirmed Instinct MI450 GPUs — featuring 432 GB of HBM4 memory and approximately 1.5x the bandwidth of NVIDIA's Rubin — are being sampled by lead customers including OpenAI and Meta. AMD is simultaneously in early engagement with customers on the successor MI500 architecture.

items.headline: AMD confirmed its Advancing AI 2026 event for July 23, expected to detail MI400 series specs
items.detail: AMD confirmed Advancing AI 2026 for July 23, 2026, expected to provide deeper technical disclosures on the MI400 series including production timelines, competitive benchmarks, and pricing — following the Computex 2026 preview. This event is a key milestone for the combined 12 GW of committed AMD GPU deployments.

items.headline: AMD acquired MEXT for predictive memory technology and signed a 30 MW Rackspace compute deployment deal
items.detail: AMD acquired MEXT on June 15 — a Santa Clara startup whose predictive memory technology makes flash storage behave like DRAM, expanding usable memory capacity for AI training and inference at scale. On June 16, AMD and Rackspace Technology signed a definitive agreement for a phased 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is now available worldwide, reaching $500M ARR as of April 2026 (up from $450M in March, 335% YoY growth) with 100 million+ monthly active users. The 'Computer' agentic tool expanded into native Microsoft 365 side panels across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams in May 2026. CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed the 2028 IPO target on June 9, citing deliberate strategy rather than market conditions. The $400 million Snap distribution deal and $750 million Microsoft Azure compute agreement provide distribution and infrastructure scale.

items.headline: Perplexity Comet browser reached worldwide availability; ARR at $500M as of April 2026
items.detail: Perplexity's Comet AI browser reached the #3 App Store position within 48 hours of its iOS launch in March 2026 and is now globally available. ARR hit $500 million in April 2026 (335% YoY growth from $148M in June 2025), with 100 million+ monthly active users. The company projects $656M ARR by year end.

items.headline: Perplexity Computer is now available as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams
items.detail: Perplexity expanded its 'Computer' agentic tool into native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook on May 28 — following its Teams launch on May 4 — allowing users to research, draft, and execute tasks inside Microsoft 365 apps. This positions Perplexity as a workplace AI layer competing alongside Microsoft Copilot.

items.headline: Perplexity CEO reaffirmed the 2028 IPO target; company valued at $20 billion with $500M ARR
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas told CNBC on June 9 that Perplexity is targeting a 2028 IPO as a deliberate strategy, regardless of Anthropic and OpenAI IPO activity. The company's most recent valuation is $20 billion (May 2026 round), with ARR at $500 million and 100M+ MAU.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) is trading around $117 on June 18 — a fourth straight day of gains (+18.87% over the week) as traders front-run Nasdaq-100 inclusion before market open on June 22, followed by Russell 3000 inclusion on June 27. A June 18 report (24/7 Wall St.) identified approximately $32 billion in new contracted commitments added since the Q1 report, pushing the total backlog toward ~$131 billion. The company's capex floor for 2026 was raised to $31 billion to accommodate Vera Rubin NVL72 additions for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI.

items.headline: CoreWeave confirmed for Nasdaq-100 entry before market open June 22; Russell 3000 June 27
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) was officially selected for the Nasdaq-100 Index effective before market open June 22, 2026, as part of a rebalance adding five AI stocks. Russell 3000 inclusion follows June 27. Both inclusions trigger structural passive buying as ETFs and index funds tracking these indices must purchase CRWV shares.

items.headline: CoreWeave Q1 2026 revenue surged 112% to $2.1B; contracted backlog approaching $131B
items.detail: CoreWeave reported Q1 2026 revenue of $2.1 billion, up 112% year over year. Its contracted revenue backlog hit $99.4 billion at end of Q1 following $40 billion+ in new commitments, with analysts projecting approximately $131 billion by end of Q2 2026. A new 15-year Chicago-area hyperscale data center lease adds $2.2 billion in contracted revenue.

items.headline: CoreWeave added ~$32 billion to its contracted backlog ahead of Nasdaq-100 inclusion
items.detail: A June 18 analysis (24/7 Wall St.) estimated CoreWeave has added roughly $32 billion in new contracted commitments since reporting $99.4 billion at end-Q1, pushing the total backlog toward ~$131 billion. The additions are driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 deployments for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. CRWV is trading around $117 on June 18 — up 18.87% over the past week — with Nasdaq-100 inclusion confirmed for June 22 and Russell 3000 for June 27.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral is in early-stage talks to raise approximately €3 billion (~$3.5B) at a valuation of about €20 billion (~$23B) — nearly doubling from its prior €11.7B mark — per Bloomberg and TechCrunch reporting from June 12. CEO Arthur Mensch appeared at the G7 Évian summit and at VivaTech 2026, positioning Mistral as Europe's sovereign AI champion following the Anthropic Fable/Mythos export suspension. NVIDIA reaffirmed Mistral as the flagship of France's sovereign compute strategy at VivaTech. ARR has crossed $400 million. The Les Ulis, France inference data center funded by €680M in debt financing remains on track for Q3 2026.

items.headline: Mistral is in talks to raise €3 billion at a ~€20 billion valuation, nearly doubling from its prior round
items.detail: Mistral AI is in early-stage discussions to raise approximately €3 billion ($3.5B) at a valuation of about €20 billion ($23B) — nearly doubling from its €11.7B September 2025 Series C valuation — per Bloomberg and TechCrunch reporting from June 12. CEO Mensch also said Mistral is exploring designing its own chips to reduce NVIDIA dependence. Terms may change; no deal is closed.

items.headline: NVIDIA reaffirmed Mistral as Europe's sovereign AI flagship at VivaTech; Mensch pitched open-source sovereignty at G7
items.detail: At VivaTech 2026, Jensen Huang reaffirmed Mistral AI as the anchor of France's sovereign AI strategy. CEO Arthur Mensch attended the G7 AI sessions in Évian and stated 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control exercised by states or corporations' — directly leveraging European alarm over the Anthropic Fable/Mythos suspension.

items.headline: Mistral's Les Ulis, France inference data center funded by €680M in debt financing remains on track for Q3 2026
items.detail: Mistral's 10MW Les Ulis (Essonne) facility dedicated to inference operations remains scheduled to open in Q3 2026, funded in part by €680 million ($830 million) in debt financing closed in March 2026. ASML is the largest shareholder at approximately 11% after its €1.3 billion investment.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Tesla expanded its unsupervised Cybercab robotaxi service to the entire Austin metro area by June 8, following expansions to Dallas and Houston in April 2026. Production Cybercab specifications became public via EPA documentation. FSD v14.3 launched in April with reinforcement learning upgrades and 20% lower inference latency. Optimus Gen 3 mass production continues at Fremont with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026; a dedicated Optimus factory is under construction at Giga Texas North Campus.

items.headline: Tesla expanded unsupervised Cybercab robotaxi service to the full Austin metro; Dallas and Houston already active
items.detail: By June 8, Tesla expanded its unsupervised Cybercab service to the entire Austin metro area. The company had launched unsupervised rides in Dallas and Houston in April 2026. Production Cybercab specs — two-passenger, butterfly doors, no door handles, no rear window, no side mirrors, hatchback cargo — became public via EPA documentation.

items.headline: FSD v14.3 launched with reinforcement learning upgrades and 20% lower inference latency
items.detail: Tesla's FSD (Supervised) v14.3 launched in April 2026 featuring upgraded reinforcement learning for edge case handling, an enhanced neural network vision encoder for low-visibility scenarios, and a rewritten AI compiler that reduced inference latency by up to 20%.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026
items.detail: Tesla began Optimus Gen 3 mass production at Fremont on January 21, 2026 with 50 actuators and 22 degrees of freedom in the hand design. V3 full-body production remains on track for summer 2026, with the Fremont Model S/X line being repurposed for Optimus and a dedicated factory under construction at Giga Texas North Campus.

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed filing; October 2026 target; Fable/Mythos export suspension now flagged as primary IPO risk
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, after closing a $65 billion Series H at a ~$965 billion post-money valuation (May 28). An October 2026 listing window is widely reported. The Fable 5/Mythos 5 export control suspension (effective June 12) remains in effect as of June 18, but co-founder Jack Clark told Bloomberg the company is in daily discussions with the Trump administration — the most concrete progress signal since the suspension. Fortune identified the government's demonstrated power to suspend model access as a novel material IPO risk Anthropic must disclose in its public S-1. G7 leaders agreed to a 'trusted partners' platform within one month with a September follow-on, but no access has been restored.
ipoTracker.note: Jack Clark's Bloomberg interview (June 18) signaling daily discussions with the Trump administration is the most positive development since the suspension. The G7 'trusted partners' framework and the daily-discussions signal together represent incremental progress, but no timeline exists for restoration.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic's IPO pitch has a new problem: The government can shut it down

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed SEC filing June 8; timing 'not yet determined'; GPT-5.6 imminent
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026. Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan are leading the offering. Reporting indicates a target valuation near $1 trillion but the company has said IPO timing has 'not yet been determined,' a more cautious posture than earlier September–Q4 2026 window reporting. GPT-5.6 — described internally as a 'meaningful improvement' — has prediction markets assigning ~83% probability to a June 22–28 release.
ipoTracker.note: OpenAI's 'timing not yet determined' comment is slightly more cautious than prior reporting; watch for GPT-5.6 launch in the late-June window as a potential pre-IPO product catalyst.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI Confidentially Files S-1 with SEC

ipoTracker.status: SPCX trading ~$191.82 on June 18; announced $60B Cursor acquisition on June 16
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares closed the first day (June 12) at $160.95 (+19%), reached an all-time high of $225.64 on June 16, then pulled back to approximately $191.82 on June 18 following a hawkish Fed signal — still up approximately 42% from the IPO price in six trading days. On June 16, just four days post-IPO, SpaceX announced the $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI). Because xAI was folded entirely into SpaceX (now operating as the SpaceXAI division) in February 2026, all Grok/AI exposure trades via ticker SPCX.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: The $60B Cursor acquisition is not yet closed; regulatory and shareholder approvals are pending. The June 18 pullback to ~$191.82 is the second consecutive down day from the $225.64 ATH.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX Confirms $60B All-Stock Acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI)

ipoTracker.status: IPO completed May 2026; Q1 2026 earnings due June 23
ipoTracker.process: Cerebras completed its Nasdaq IPO in May 2026 under ticker CBRS. As of mid-June the stock trades around $218, with Q1 2026 financial results scheduled for release after market close on June 23, 2026 — Cerebras' first quarterly report as a public company. The earnings call is at 2 pm PT / 5 pm ET on June 23.
ipoTracker.note: The June 23 Q1 earnings release is the next major catalyst and will be closely watched for growth trajectory and AI infrastructure demand signals.

latestArticle.title: Cerebras Systems Sets Date of First-Quarter 2026 Financial Results

ipoTracker.status: CRWV trading ~$117 on June 18; joining Nasdaq-100 June 22 and Russell 3000 June 27; ~$32B in new backlog commitments reported June 18
ipoTracker.process: CoreWeave completed its IPO in March 2025 (priced at $40/share) and trades on Nasdaq as CRWV. Shares are trading around $117 on June 18 (+18.87% over the past week), ahead of Nasdaq-100 inclusion before market open June 22 and Russell 3000 June 27. Q1 2026 revenue was $2.1 billion (+112% YoY). Contracted backlog has grown by approximately $32 billion since end-Q1, approaching ~$131 billion total. 2026 capex floor raised to $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 additions.
ipoTracker.note: Back-to-back index inclusions (Nasdaq-100 June 22, Russell 3000 June 27) create near-term passive-fund buying pressure. The $131B backlog and Q1 revenue growth trajectory make CRWV the strongest post-IPO AI infrastructure comps alongside SPCX and CBRS.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Soars 10% Toward Nasdaq-100 Inclusion as AI Backlog Nears $131 Billion

ipoTracker.status: No filing; IPO deferred to 2027 or later; $134B valuation, 65% YoY revenue growth
ipoTracker.process: Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi reiterated in early June that going public is 'absolutely not happening in 2026,' citing the SpaceX/Anthropic/OpenAI trifecta absorbing market capital. The company has a $5.4 billion annualized revenue run-rate growing 65% YoY with positive free cash flow and a $134 billion valuation from its December 2025 Series L. Analysts expect a possible S-1 filing as early as Q3 2026 even with the listing itself pushed to 2027+.
ipoTracker.note: No change from prior check; 2027+ remains the expected listing window.

latestArticle.title: Databricks CEO says company plans IPO despite current market conditions

ipoTracker.status: Unverified filing claim; no primary-source confirmation
ipoTracker.process: Some IPO tracker sites continue to claim Scale AI filed an S-1 in March 2026, but no matching SEC filing, Scale AI announcement, or confirmation from credible outlets (Reuters, Bloomberg, CNBC) has been found as of June 18, 2026. Scale raised more than $14 billion in June 2025 and is unlikely to pursue a near-term IPO. Speculative estimates put a possible listing between 2026 and 2028.
ipoTracker.confidence: Low until verified by primary source
ipoTracker.note: No change from prior check; no SEC EDGAR record for Scale AI has been found.

ipoTracker.stage: High-growth private company — 2028 IPO target confirmed
ipoTracker.status: No confirmed filing; CEO reaffirmed 2028 target; $20B valuation, $500M ARR
ipoTracker.process: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed the 2028 IPO target on June 9, 2026, as a deliberate strategy. ARR has reached $500 million (April 2026), up 335% YoY, with 100 million+ monthly active users. The Comet browser launched globally, the 'Computer' agentic tool expanded into Microsoft 365 native panels, and the $400 million Snap distribution deal and $750 million Microsoft Azure compute agreement provide scale ahead of a future listing.
ipoTracker.note: M365 native panel integration and $500M ARR are the most significant updates since the prior check; 2028 target unchanged.

latestArticle.title: Perplexity plans for 2028 IPO debut

ipoTracker.process: Aggregator blogs claim Hugging Face debuted on Nasdaq under ticker HFCE at $42/share in June 2026, but this cannot be verified through Hugging Face's official channels, the SEC, Reuters, or Bloomberg. Pre-IPO trading platform listings quoting Hugging Face shares at prices inconsistent with the claimed $42 IPO price directly contradict the claim. Treat as fabricated or premature until confirmed by a primary source.

partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceXAI (formerly xAI, now the AI division of publicly traded SpaceX) signed an agreement to provide Anthropic access to Colossus 1 compute capacity, with Anthropic expressing interest in developing multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity. Public financial terms remain undisclosed. SPCX's $60B acquisition of Cursor (pending close) is ongoing. On the Grok distribution side, Grok 4.3 went GA on Amazon Bedrock (June 15) and Grok became natively available on Databricks Agent Bricks (June 18) — expanding SpaceXAI's enterprise AI footprint independently of the Anthropic compute partnership.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed relationship; financial terms not confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: Grok 4.3 on Bedrock and Grok on Databricks (both this week) are significant new distribution milestones for SpaceXAI's model business, distinct from the Colossus compute partnership with Anthropic.

latestArticle.title: New Compute Partnership with Anthropic

partnershipTracker.terms: The April 2026 expanded agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years, securing up to 5GW of Trainium2, Trainium3, and Trainium4 capacity for training and running Claude. Project Rainier already runs more than one million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads. Amazon confirmed Claude remains available to AWS customers outside direct DoD work even as the Pentagon supply chain dispute continues. AWS Summit NYC's AgentCore GA (June 17–18) deepens the enterprise deployment layer for Claude on AWS.
partnershipTracker.note: AgentCore GA with Managed Knowledge Base, Web Search, and Agent Optimization announced at AWS Summit NYC reinforces AWS Bedrock as the enterprise deployment platform for Claude workloads.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic and Amazon expand collaboration for up to 5 gigawatts of new compute

partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic's agreement with Google and Broadcom secures approximately 3.5 gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity expected to start coming online in 2027. Google separately announced an investment of up to $40 billion in Anthropic (April 24). Broadcom reconfirmed Anthropic as one of its six core custom-silicon customers on its Q2 FY2026 call, guiding Q3 AI chip revenue to ~$16 billion.
partnershipTracker.note: No new terms announced this week; the 2027 capacity timeline and Google's $40B investment are the current structural anchors.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, investment, and GPU/CPU optimization
partnershipTracker.terms: Anthropic has committed to purchase $30 billion of Azure compute capacity (with option for 1GW more). Claude Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5, and Opus 4.1 are now in public preview in Microsoft Foundry. NVIDIA's Vera CPUs are in production at Anthropic. Work IQ APIs went GA June 16, enabling richer enterprise agent deployments that can include Claude on Azure.
partnershipTracker.note: Claude models entering Foundry public preview is the most relevant recent development — expanding Anthropic's enterprise distribution on Azure beyond direct API access.

latestArticle.title: Vera Arrives: NVIDIA's First CPU Built for Agents Lands at Top AI Labs

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, product distribution, and IP licensing
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed amended partnership; OpenAI exploring breach-of-contract claim over Apple placement
partnershipTracker.terms: Microsoft remains OpenAI's primary cloud partner under a non-exclusive IP license through 2032. GPT-5.5 reached general availability in Microsoft Foundry on June 3. Work IQ APIs and Web IQ APIs went GA June 16 — both power Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT's web grounding, underscoring continued deep technical integration even as Microsoft diversifies its model lineup with MAI-Code-1-Flash and MAI-Thinking-1.
partnershipTracker.note: GPT-5.5 GA in Foundry and Web IQ GA are the most recent developments. OpenAI's potential breach-of-contract action against Apple over ChatGPT placement in Siri is a notable tension point.

latestArticle.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership

partnershipTracker.type: Cloud, model distribution, and strategic infrastructure partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed strategic partnership — GA distribution live; AgentCore GA at AWS Summit reinforces
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI's frontier models (GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4) and Codex are generally available on AWS Bedrock at first-party pricing since June 1. OpenAI also holds a 2GW Trainium capacity contract. AgentCore GA (June 17) provides an enterprise agent runtime hosting OpenAI-powered agents alongside Claude and other models on Bedrock.
partnershipTracker.note: AgentCore GA at AWS Summit (June 17–18) reinforces Bedrock as the enterprise distribution layer for OpenAI models alongside Claude. No new contract terms.

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed strategic partnership — Vera CPUs now live at OpenAI
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA agreed to deploy at least 10GW of NVIDIA systems for OpenAI infrastructure, with the first gigawatt targeted for H2 2026 on Vera Rubin. NVIDIA's Vera CPUs are now in production at OpenAI. Jensen Huang at VivaTech (June 17–18) highlighted European AI factory expansion including infrastructure serving OpenAI workloads.
partnershipTracker.note: Vera Rubin cloud deployments in H2 2026 remain the next major milestone; no new contract terms.

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed strategic partnership — MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI
partnershipTracker.terms: OpenAI and AMD agreed on a multi-generation 6GW AMD GPU deployment beginning with an initial MI450 rollout in H2 2026. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to OpenAI. A 160-million-share AMD warrant vests on milestone achievement. Citi upgraded AMD to Buy ($575 target) on June 12, citing this partnership and the Meta deal as key drivers.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by company; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: AMD's Advancing AI 2026 event on July 23 may include additional OpenAI deployment disclosures. H2 2026 production deployment timeline on track.

partnershipTracker.terms: Stargate spans Oracle (up to 4.5GW, $300B+ over five years), SoftBank (two sites scaling to 1.5GW), and CoreWeave (cloud partnership totaling up to $22.4 billion across three expansions). CoreWeave's pending Nasdaq-100 inclusion (June 22) and Russell 3000 (June 27) add passive-fund visibility to the Stargate infrastructure network. The OpenAI-SoftBank Japan enterprise event was held June 16.
partnershipTracker.note: CoreWeave's imminent index inclusions are the primary contextual development; no new Stargate site announcements this week.

partnershipTracker.terms: CoreWeave provides Meta AI cloud capacity through December 2032 under a roughly $21 billion agreement, including NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 deployments for Meta's Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp inference workloads. This contract is among the anchor commitments driving CoreWeave's backlog approaching $131 billion.
partnershipTracker.note: No new terms announced; CoreWeave's Nasdaq-100 inclusion and backlog trajectory reflect the growing scale of this contract.

Financial terms remain undisclosed. CoreWeave's approaching $131 billion backlog includes the Anthropic contract; Vera Rubin NVL72 capability is the key enabler for future Claude deployments under this agreement.
partnershipTracker.note: No new terms announced; CoreWeave's Nasdaq-100 inclusion and backlog growth are contextually relevant to this contract's scale.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Announces Multi-Year Agreement With Anthropic

partnershipTracker.status: Active under strain — Pentagon migrated two-thirds of AI workflows off Claude; Maven remains DoD program of record
partnershipTracker.terms: Claude runs inside Palantir's Maven Smart System on AWS infrastructure for US government intelligence and defense users. The relationship remains under acute strain: the Pentagon has reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of its AI workflows off Anthropic's models amid the supply chain risk designation dispute. Palantir CEO Karp confirmed Claude remains integrated in Maven; Maven was simultaneously designated an official DoD program of record. Amazon confirmed Claude remains available to AWS customers outside direct DoD work.
partnershipTracker.note: The Pentagon's two-thirds workflow migration off Anthropic is the most significant ongoing development. The Fable/Mythos suspension adds additional complexity to any near-term resolution.

latestArticle.title: Pentagon designates Palantir supply chain risk threatening multibillion-dollar defense contracts

partnershipTracker.status: Active but strained — Extensions framework built but disabled; OpenAI exploring breach-of-contract action
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple and OpenAI maintain an existing integration bringing ChatGPT into Siri and Apple Intelligence. The iOS 27 developer beta contains a working Extensions framework — letting users choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri — but Apple has disabled it on the backend. OpenAI is reportedly exploring a breach-of-contract action over perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI architecture. No firm date exists for enabling Extensions.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apple; Extensions confirmed built but not yet publicly enabled; breach-of-contract claim reported but not confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: The potential OpenAI breach-of-contract action is the most significant new development in this relationship; it could delay or reshape the Extensions launch.

latestArticle.title: Apple Hides Third-Party Siri Extensions in iOS 27 Beta

partnershipTracker.terms: AMD MI300X GPU instances are deployed on Azure for AI inference workloads alongside NVIDIA hardware. Work IQ APIs went GA June 16, potentially driving more enterprise agent workloads onto Azure infrastructure where AMD silicon competes for inference capacity. AMD's July 23 Advancing AI 2026 event may include additional Azure-related partnership disclosures.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Ongoing relationship; no new announced terms this week

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed 6GW, ~$60 billion multi-year agreement — MI450 samples now shipping
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta's 6-gigawatt, roughly $60 billion strategic partnership covers multiple generations of AMD Instinct GPUs co-developed via the Helios architecture through the Open Compute Project, with shipments beginning H2 2026. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta. The agreement includes a 160-million-share AMD warrant giving Meta a potential 10% stake. Meta's updated 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion reflects the scale of this and other AI infrastructure commitments.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: MI450 samples shipping to Meta confirmed; H2 2026 production deployment on track. Citi's June 12 AMD upgrade to Buy cited this partnership as a key driver.

partnershipTracker.type: Infrastructure equity investment and multi-generational GPU supply
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed deep strategic partnership — Vera Rubin NVL72 live; backlog approaching $131 billion
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA invested roughly $2 billion in CoreWeave Class A common stock (~11% stake) and the companies committed to accelerating more than 5GW of AI factories by 2030. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up on June 1. The AI compute backlog is approaching $131 billion (analyst estimate) as of June 18, driven by Vera Rubin NVL72 commitments from Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI. CoreWeave joins the Nasdaq-100 on June 22, raising the partnership's profile with passive investors.
partnershipTracker.note: Nasdaq-100 inclusion June 22 is the next major milestone; the approaching $131B backlog reflects the commercial scale of the Vera Rubin NVL72 deployment pipeline.
